solution.answer: $L(7,1)$ and $L(7,2)$ have the same homology but are not homeomorphic: $2 \not\equiv \pm 1^{\pm 1} \pmod 7$.
steps: Both spaces have $H_*(L(7,q)) = (\mathbb{Z}, \mathbb{Z}/7, 0, \mathbb{Z})$ for $q = 1, 2$. Integral homology cannot distinguish them.
The linking form: for the generator $[\gamma]$ of $H_1(L(p,q)) = \mathbb{Z}/p$, the linking form satisfies $\lambda([\gamma],[\gamma]) = q/p \in \mathbb{Q}/\mathbb{Z}$.

An isometry of linking forms preserving orientation requires $1/7 \equiv \pm(n^2 \cdot 2/7) \pmod{1}$ for some integer $n$, i.e.\ $1 \equiv \pm 2n^2 \pmod{7}$. Checking: $2n^2 \pmod{7}$ takes values $\{0,2,1,4\}$ for $n=0,1,2,3$; neither $+1$ nor $-1 \equiv 6$ appear as $2n^2$.

The homeomorphism criterion: $L(p,q) \cong L(p,q')$ iff $q' \equiv \pm q^{\pm 1} \pmod p$. For $(p,q)=(7,1)$ and $q'=2$: $\pm 1^{\pm 1} \equiv \pm 1 \equiv 1$ or $6 \pmod 7$. Since $2 \neq 1, 6$, the spaces are not homeomorphic.
This is the classical example showing homology is insufficient to classify lens spaces; one needs Reidemeister torsion or the linking form.
